name: The Futurist Case for AI: Industry, Ethics, And Human Identity
description:
Facilitator(s)


Steven Maheshwary is a business and technology leader with a passion for AI and a background spanning public and private sectors. He formerly served as Governor Inslee's Technology Sector Lead, chartered with growing Washington's $100B tech and AI economy, and has been a spokesperson on responsible and ethical AI development (years before the advent of gen AI). Steven is currently a product strategy and growth leader with a background in commercializing and scaling billion-dollar tech businesses across his time at Amazon, Microsoft, and startups prior. In addition, he serves as an advisor to AI-native startups and civic tech organizations, including formerly chairing Seattle's Technology Advisory Board and serving as a board director for the Washington Technology Industry Association. He is an AI-empowered father, husband, Harvard alum, and Fulbright Scholar and hails from Houston, TX.


Description


Discover how artificial intelligence is reshaping our world—and our sense of self. In this session, we’ll explore emerging AI-driven breakthroughs revolutionizing entire industries, examine ethical considerations, and delve into how shifts in technology will influence our identity at work and beyond.


Additional Details


This event is part of the AI Workshop Series, which runs every Friday at 1:00pm from April 11th - May 30th (on Zoom) and is brought to you by Microsoft, the Center of Excellence for Information & Computing Technology, Computing For All, and Bellevue College Computer Science.


The AI Workshop Series is open to all community and technical college students across Washington state.

Note: Students must register using their college email address.

name: Coding with Bolt: AI Workshop for Non-Engineers
description:
Description:


Please note: This event is IN PERSON.


Build your own app in under 3 hours using Bolt's AI coding tools (no coding experience required). Confirmed participants will receive a free one-month subscription to Bolt Pro at the event to help them build their app!


Registration Steps:


  1. Click on the registration link to visit the event page.
  2. Scroll down on the event page and read the Important registration information
  3. Click the Request to Join button
  4. Create a Bolt account using the referral link


Additional Details:


Event capacity and waitlist management:


?There is a 50 participant capacity for seating and free Bolt Pro subscriptions


?If you are approved for the event, you need to check-in by the start of event to guarantee your spot, so show up a little early.


?Anyone approved for the waitlist should still show up to the event because:


?If any approved participants don’t show up on time, people will be removed from the waitlist and those present will be checked-in to the event on a first-come, first-serve basis.


?Anyone who doesn't get off the waitlist can still hang around and learn with a free account!

name: AWS West Virtual Job Fair Spring 2025
description:
Description:


Amazon Web Services (AWS) and Year Up United (YUU) are inviting you to join them for a Tech Talent Virtual Job Fair on Thursday, April 24th. Learn about and apply for internships and full-time roles. Companies are looking for individuals with the skills to get the job done – like you! This event will be a great opportunity for you to engage with employers that are actively looking for tech talent! Upload your resume and get ready to connect.


Additional Details:


This session is presented by Amazon Web Services (AWS) Skills to Jobs and Year Up United, who have teamed up to amplify the impact of the AWS Skills to Jobs Tech Alliance within the U.S. The Tech Alliance is a national coalition of employers, educational institutions, and government agencies that addresses the skills gap and prepares learners for in-demand tech jobs. Year Up United, a nationally lauded organization has “catalyzed change in the talent and business landscape for nearly 25 years”.

name: Seattle Virtual Job Fair
description:
DISCOVER YOUR NEXT CAREER OPPORTUNITY AT OUR VIRTUAL JOB FAIR

Are you currently seeking employment in the vibrant city of Seattle? Look no further! Register now and join us at the highly anticipated Seattle Virtual Job Fair. With a proven track record of organizing successful hiring events nationwide, Best Hire Career Fairs guarantees an unparalleled experience.

What sets us apart from other job fair companies is our unique ability to understand employers' requirements and connect them with top-tier candidates. Whether you are looking to secure a new job or advance your career, this event is not to be missed. Mark your calendar for June 18th, between 10 a.m. and 2 p.m., so you can engage directly with hiring managers and recruiters from leading companies.


PREPARE FOR A REMARKABLE EXPERIENCE AT THE VIRTUAL SEATTLE JOB FAIRS

A virtual job fair offers an exceptional opportunity to meet with decision-making hiring managers in your area. You no longer need to waste countless hours submitting resumes without receiving any responses. Take this chance to update your resume and bring your best self forward – it's time to stand out truly.


NO COSTS INVOLVED FOR ATTENDING BEST HIRE VIRTUAL JOB FAIRS

As a job seeker, attending this event comes at absolutely no cost! If you are an employer interested in participating as a hiring company at our job fair, please don't hesitate to contact us toll-free at (888) 899-8802. Our dedicated team members will provide all the necessary information for registration.


IMPORTANT DETAILS ABOUT THE SEATTLE VIRTUAL JOB FAIR


JOB FAIR LOCATION

Virtual - Online


TIME OF THIS EVENT

10:00 am – 2:00 pm


EMPLOYERS LOOKING TO ATTEND THIS EVENT

If you are an employer interested in attending, please call us toll-free at (888) 899-8802 or visit our Seattle Job Fairs webpage for more information.


INDUSTRIES THAT HIRE AT OUR SEATTLE JOB FAIRS

Accommodations, Accounting, Advertising, Aerospace, Agriculture & Agribusiness, Air Transportation, Apparel & Accessories, Auto, Banking, Beauty & Cosmetics, Biotechnology, Chemical, Communications, Computer, Construction, Consulting, Consumer Products, Education, Electronics, Employment, Energy, Entertainment & Recreation, Fashion, Financial Services, Fine Arts, Food & Beverage, Green Technology, Health, Information, Information Technology, Insurance, Journalism & News, Legal Services, Manufacturing, Media & Broadcasting, Medical Devices & Supplies, Motion Pictures & Video, Music, Pharmaceutical, Public Administration, Public Relations, Publishing, Real Estate, Retail, Service, Sports, Technology, Telecommunications, Tourism, Transportation, Travel, Utilities, Video Game, Web Services.

name: A "Human" Perspective: Building Products & Careers
description:
Join Melroy D'Souza, former UX Director at Microsoft and current Guest Faculty Member at the UW iSchool, as he shares key insights and lessons learned from his tech industry journey. He'll reveal the mistakes he made and how to avoid them, empowering you to make a smooth and successful transition.

This event is brought to you by Computing For All (CFA), the backbone organization of the Tech Workforce Coalition (TWC). CFA offers monthly workshops facilitated by industry experts to equip you with the skills and insights needed to launch or advance your career in the technology sector.

name: Beyond the Hype: How Companies Use AI Today
description:
Facilitator(s)


Praful Konduru is a Senior Software Engineer with over a decade of experience at leading technology companies, including Meta, Amazon, PayPal, Venmo, Dell, and Salesforce. He specializes in AI-driven automation, scalable distributed systems, cloud computing, and high-performance engineering solutions.


Description


AI is everywhere—but what’s real, and what’s just buzz? In this session, we’ll break down what AI is (in simple terms) and explore real-world case studies from leading companies like Amazon, Meta, and Salesforce. Learn how AI is driving innovation across industries and what it means for the future of work.


Additional Details


This event is part of the AI Workshop Series, which runs every Friday at 1:00pm from April 11th - May 30th (on Zoom) and is brought to you by Microsoft, the Center of Excellence for Information & Computing Technology, Computing For All, and Bellevue College Computer Science.


The AI Workshop Series is open to all community and technical college students across Washington state.

Note: Students must register using their college email address.

name: YUPRO Jobseeker Bootcamp Series: Job Search Strategies
description:
The YUPRO Placement Jobseeker Bootcamp series includes daily 45-minute trainings on Tuesday- Friday from 1-2 pm ET (10 am PT) over Zoom.


It is not required to attend every session; you can select sessions to attend throughout the week.


Trainings are open to all members of YUPRO Placement’s talent community and are facilitated by YUPRO Placement staff members. All participants will have the opportunity to network with jobseekers around the country, receive feedback on job search materials (resume, LinkedIn profile, interview responses, etc.), learn new technologies to support your search including Teal, Huntr, and Jobscan.


  1. Tuesday: 10 -10:45 pm PST, Job Search Strategies
  2. Wednesday: 10 -10:45 pm PST, Resume Creation
  3. Thursday: 10 -10:45 pm PST, LinkedIn & Networking
  4. Friday: 10 -10:45 pm PST, Interview Preparation & Salary Negotiation

name: Elevate Your Career: Tips & Tricks from Brooksource
description:
As part of the AWS Skills to Jobs Tech Alliance event series for Spring 2025, AWS is partnering with the job-search experts at Brooksource to offer Elevate Your Career: Tips & Tricks from Brooksource.


In this session, the job search experts at Brooksource will give attendees practical tips on:

  1. Nailing a virtual interview
  2. Building a resume that gets you noticed
  3. Maximizing your reach through networking
  4. Taking a proactive approach to your job search

This session is a must-go for students that will be attending the upcoming virtual job fairs or even any local career/job fairs. When we have held these sessions previously, students’ feedback has been overwhelmingly positive. Please use the invite template below to encourage your students and recent graduates to attend.

name: Microsoft Copilot – Design a dream destination with AI
description:
Facilitator(s)


Liz Butowicz is the Founder of Black Dog Black Cat, a Microsoft Training Services Partner. She brings her extensive expertise and an agile team of professionals to empower educators and institutions with the essential knowledge to effectively teach artificial intelligence (AI), cloud computing, and productivity technology skills.


Through virtual and in-person training Black Dog Black Cat increases the number of faculty and educators who are well-prepared to deliver Microsoft AI skills to students. Faculty and educators can then empower students with the tech skills needed to succeed in an AI-driven job market.


With over 10 years as an education consultant and 20 years of industry experience at Microsoft, Liz has made a positive impact on thousands of students and educators worldwide. Her education initiatives enable teachers to confidently teach technology skills in the classroom. In her free time, Liz grows oysters on Whidbey Island, Washington.


Description


Join us for a hands-on workshop where you will learn about the capabilities of generative AI using Microsoft Copilot. Bring your personal creativity and passion to dream up a novel destination and create the content to help tell its story.


Additional Details


This event is part of the AI Workshop Series, which runs every Friday at 1:00pm from April 11th - May 30th (on Zoom) and is brought to you by Microsoft, the Center of Excellence for Information & Computing Technology, Computing For All, and Bellevue College Computer Science.


The AI Workshop Series is open to all community and technical college students across Washington state.

Note: Students must register using their college email address.

name: Game Changers: AI and the Future of Live Sports (hosted by IBM SkillsBuild)
description:
Description:


We are thrilled to invite you to be part of this engaging webinar featuring a leading expert at the intersection of AI and sports, provided by YUPRO and their partnership with IBM SkillsBuild! Discover how cutting-edge AI technologies are transforming live sports and player selection. Learn how IBM SkillsBuild can help you upskill or reskill in AI, opening doors to exciting career opportunities. This session is designed to spark your curiosity and empower you to take the next step in your learning journey. Don’t miss this opportunity to connect and learn.


Additional Details:


Registration is limited to the first 1,000 people.


Each webinar will be recorded and posted to IBM SkillsBuild.

name: Recruiting 101: Navigating LinkedIn, Resumes, Interviews, and Beyond
description:
Join Facilitator Mikayla Sullivan, University Programs Manager at Smartsheet, as she shares how to navigate LinkedIn, resume tips and tricks, networking strategies for career fairs, and best practices for preparing for interviews. Participants will also have the opportunity to learn more about Smartsheet and their hiring process!


This event is part of our Career Services Workshop Series, designed to provide you with the tools and knowledge needed to excel in your career.

name: IBM SkillsBuild Webinar: Elevate your Resume with the Power of AI
description:
In partnership with SkillUp Online, an IBM training delivery vendor, the IBM SkillsBuild team is announcing a new webinar session, Transform Your Resume with AI.


No experience required, here are the details:


Registration Period: Now - Apr 29th

Webinar date: Apr 29th

Webinar Time: 7am PT - 8am PT

Language: English


This is a skill-building opportunity.

During the webinar session you will:

Learn about the transformative role of generative AI and software development.

Leverage IBM Granite LLM for AI-enhanced content creation.

Apply new skills through real-world applications.

name: Tech & Business Networking
description:
Key Details:

  1. Age: 21+
  2. The event is from 6:00 PM - 8:00 PM (feel free to come at any point in this timeframe).
  3. Name tags will be provided.
  4. Business casual dress code. Job fair attire.


Are you interested in tech and business? Want to elevate your career? Meet Consultants, Analysts of all types, Bankers, Software Engineers, Entrepreneurs, and more who are active in the space or are eager to get started.


**This will be a multi-vertical event**

There will be professionals with backgrounds in the arts, finance, tech, real estate, healthcare, and more. Come with an open mind, and leave with connections that wouldn't be possible otherwise!


Pack some business cards and come with an open mind!

Meet your future business partners, employers, coworkers, investors, and friends. You don't want to miss this opportunity to expand your network and create a brighter future.


Remember - it's not about who you know today, it's who you'll meet tomorrow!


We look forward to meeting you at the event!


Sincerely,

Strive Networking Team
location: Comet Tavern: 922 E Pike St, Seattle, WA 98122

name: Learn Live: Develop an AI Agent with Semantic Kernel
description:
Topic: Core AI

Language: English


This module provides engineers with the skills to begin building Azure AI Agent Service agents with Semantic Kernel.


Learning objectives:

By the end of this module, you'll be able to:

  1. Use Semantic Kernel to connect to an Azure AI Foundry project.
  2. Azure AI Agent Service agents using the Semantic Kernel SDK.
  3. Integrate plugin functions with your AI agent.

name: Developing Essential Skills
description:
Learn from Sathya Sindhu Varadarajan, Product Owner at Bank of America, as she empowers you to take control of your career journey. Understand how to leverage self-awareness, build powerful networks, set impactful goals, and navigate the job search process with confidence. Discover proven interview strategies and stay ahead of the curve in your industry.


This event is brought to you by Computing For All (CFA), the backbone organization of the Tech Workforce Coalition (TWC). CFA offers monthly opportunities to gain Professional Perspectives through expert-led sessions, designed to equip you with the skills and insights needed to launch or advance your career in the technology sector.

name: YUPRO Jobseeker Bootcamp Series: Resume Creation
Learn Live: Integrate Custom Tools into Your Agent
description:
Topic: Core AI

Language: English


Built-in tools are useful, but they may not meet all your needs. In this module, learn how to extend the capabilities of your agent by integrating custom tools for your agent to use.


Learning objectives

By the end of this module, you'll be able to:

  1. Describe the benefits of using custom tools with your agent.
  2. Explore the different options for custom tools.
  3. Build an agent that integrates custom tools using the Azure AI Agent Service.

Follow along with the content

  1. Integrate custom tools into your agent


Explore more resources

  1. Build secure and responsible AI solutions and manage generative AI lifecycles
  2. Create agentic AI solutions by using Azure AI Foundry
  3. Practical Foundation for AI Agents: A Developer's Guide on Azure AI Foundry, Apps, and Data

name: Accelerating Your Career Growth: From Technical Excellence to Leadership
description:
Join Tejas Ghadge, Senior Software Development Manager at AWS Lambda, as he guides you from technical mastery to leadership. Acing technical skills is just the first step in a successful career. To break into senior roles, it's crucial to develop leadership qualities and personal traits that build trust and influence. This workshop explores how to strategically shape your early years to fast-track your growth beyond coding and system design into high-impact leadership positions. Tejas will share some of his mistakes and learnings that you can benefit and apply in your personal career to speed up your personal growth.


This event is brought to you by Computing For All (CFA), the backbone organization of the Tech Workforce Coalition (TWC). CFA offers monthly workshops facilitated by industry experts to equip you with the skills and insights needed to launch or advance your career in the technology sector.

name: Ask the Experts: How to Unlock Your Cybersecurity Career
description:
"Ask the Experts: How to Unlock Your Cybersecurity Career," is designed by Okta to provide actionable insights for anyone looking to launch or advance their career in cybersecurity. Ready to launch your cybersecurity career? This session features an expert panel of Okta recruiters and hiring managers from both technical and non-technical domains, along with an early career focus.


This session will feature an expert panel of Okta recruiters and hiring managers from both technical and non-technical domains, with a special focus on early career professionals. Meet Lead Technical Recruiters focused on bringing technical talent to Okta, including a Senior University Recruiter focused on hiring both new grads and interns.


During this session, participants will:

  1. Learn actionable strategies for crafting standout resumes and LinkedIn profiles.
  2. Gain invaluable tips and tricks for navigating the hiring process in cybersecurity.
  3. Hear directly from industry professionals about their experiences and the diverse paths available.

name: Building Your Personal Brand
description:
Join facilitator Prerna Grover, Sr. Technical Project Manager for IT Delivery at T-Mobile, as she shares professional insights on how to build a personal brand.


This event is part of Computing for All's Career Services Workshop Series, designed to provide you with the tools and knowledge needed to excel in your career.

name: Getting Hands-On with AI Use Cases - AI Tools for Career Development
description:
Facilitator(s)


Led by leaders of Women Defining AI, an organization working to level the playing field for women in tech and enabling a new generation of builders.


Description


The job market is rapidly changing with the rise of AI, and the best way to learn and upskill beyond the classroom is to learn-by-doing. From prepping for job interviews, updating resumes, to building an AI projects portfolio - get hands-on with our co-instructors in this workshop, and learn different ways AI can support and enhance your learning and your work both in and out of the classroom. This workshop is interactive. You'll experiment and test out prompts and LLMs alongside your co-instructors during the workshop & hopefully walk away with some powerful new tools and use cases for your own personal and professional goals.


Additional Details


This event is part of the AI Workshop Series, which runs every Friday at 1:00pm from April 11th - May 30th (on Zoom) and is brought to you by Microsoft, the Center of Excellence for Information & Computing Technology, Computing For All, and Bellevue College Computer Science.

name: 10x10: Jumpstart Your AI Journey
description:
Facilitator(s)


Devindra Chainani is a technology leader with over 30 years of experience in the tech industry, including more than two decades at Microsoft. Currently serving as Director of Technical Program Management for GPU Infrastructure at Oracle, Devindra is deeply engaged in the development of AI infrastructure. He holds multiple patents in the field and is passionate about expanding access to learning through innovation. A master’s candidate at the Harvard Graduate School of Education, Devindra also served on the board of the University of Washington’s Information School and as Co President for the Seattle chapter of Pratham.org which supports innovations in education.


Description


Master the tools that will power the future of work!

This session will give a practical guide to understanding AI tools and how they can be used to advance your education and career. Our speaker will walk you through a 10-day plan to jumpstart your AI readiness.

name: Hiring in the AI Age: How Companies Use Tech to Find and Manage Employees
description:
Facilitator(s)


Aaron Terrazas, Independent Macro and Labor Economist


Description


AI is changing the workplace before you even get hired. Explore how businesses use AI to recruit, hire, and manage employees, and what you can do to adapt in an evolving job market.


Additional Details


This event is part of the AI Workshop Series, which runs every Friday at 1:00pm from April 11th - May 30th (on Zoom) and is brought to you by Microsoft, the Center of Excellence for Information & Computing Technology, Computing For All, and Bellevue College Computer Science.


The AI Workshop Series is open to all community and technical college students across Washington state.

Note: Students must register using their college email address.

name: YUPRO Jobseeker Bootcamp Series: Interview Preparation & Salary Negotiation
Tech Employer Career Panel Spring 2025
description:
Join Amazon Web Services (AWS) Skills to Jobs and Year Up United for a 90-minute session focused on careers in the tech industry, the current state of hiring, and how you can best position yourself as a top applicant. Panelists represent Allegis Group, Apex Systems, AWS, DLZP Group, Exelon Corp, JPMorgan Chase, NetApp & Salesforce.


This session is presented by Amazon Web Services (AWS) Skills to Jobs and Year Up United, who have teamed up to amplify the impact of the AWS Skills to Jobs Tech Alliance within the U.S. The Tech Alliance is a national coalition of employers, educational institutions, and government agencies that addresses the skills gap and prepares learners for in-demand tech jobs. Year Up United, a nationally lauded non-profit organization has “catalyzed change in the talent and business landscape for nearly 25 years”.

name: YUPRO Jobseeker Bootcamp Series: LinkedIn & Networking
Learn Live: Orchestrate a Multi-agent Solution Using Semantic Kernel
description:
Topic: Core AI

Language: English


Learn how to use the Semantic Kernel SDK to develop your own AI agents that can collaborate for a multi-agent solution.


Learning objectives

By the end of this module, you'll be able to:

  1. Build AI agents using the Semantic Kernel SDK
  2. Develop multi-agent solutions
  3. Create custom selection and termination strategies for agent collaboration

Follow along with the content

  1. Orchestrate a multi-agent solution using Semantic Kernel

Explore more resources

  1. Build secure and responsible AI solutions and manage generative AI lifecycles
  2. Create agentic AI solutions by using Azure AI Foundry
  3. Practical Foundation for AI Agents: A Developer's Guide on Azure AI Foundry, Apps, and Data


Speakers:

Korey Stegared-Pace has a passion for both educating and inspiring others on what is possible with AI and how they can use it to build a new generation of products. He has mentored and guided startups through both the challenges and successes of working with AI. As a Cloud Advocate in the Microsoft Stockholm Reactor, his main goal is to expand AI literacy, support developers, and grow the AI community in Stockholm and beyond.


Shivam Goyal is a driven AI and Machine Learning engineer with deep expertise in Python, C++, and Java. He excels at crafting innovative models that align with business goals, enabling organizations to monitor and achieve their objectives efficiently. With a talent for dissecting complex algorithms, Shivam delivers impactful solutions that seamlessly connect technology with real-world business challenges. As a Microsoft Student Learn Ambassador, he is a dynamic force in the tech community, leading workshops, authoring blogs, and sparking meaningful conversations to inspire and educate others. A committed moderator of the Microsoft AI Discord community and a contributor to Microsoft Tech Community blogs, Shivam is dedicated to pushing the boundaries of technology and nurturing a collaborative learning environment.

name: Learn Live: Develop an agent with Azure AI Agent Service
description:
Topic: Core AI

Language: English


This module provides engineers with the skills to begin building agents with Azure AI Agent Service.


Learning objectives

By the end of this module, you'll be able to:

  1. Describe the purpose of AI agents
  2. Explain the key features of Azure AI Agent Service
  3. Build an agent using the Azure AI Agent Service
  4. Integrate an agent in the Azure AI Agent Service into your own application

Follow along with the content

  1. Develop an AI agent with Azure AI Agent Service


Explore more resources

  1. Build secure and responsible AI solutions and manage generative AI lifecycles
  2. Create agentic AI solutions by using Azure AI Foundry
  3. Practical Foundation for AI Agents: A Developer's Guide on Azure AI Foundry, Apps, and Data

name: Microsoft Copilot Masterclass: Enhancing Productivity for Public Sector
description:
Join this 45-minute LinkedIn Live session and learn how to craft prompts to boost productivity and drive innovation in your public sector organization. Discover best practices, real-world applications, and how to elevate your public sector solutions!


In just 45 minutes, you’ll:


  1. Learn the recipe to craft effective prompts
  2. Hear how Microsoft 365 Copilot is helping public sector organizations increase efficiency
  3. See live demonstration of relevant prompts for public sector
  4. Walk away with resources to help you along your AI transformation journey


Speakers:


Shyvee Shi, AI enthusiast and LinkedIn influencer with 100K+ followers, leads the AI Community Learning Program at Microsoft Teams. With deep expertise in AI-focused events, she brings sharp insights and practical takeaways to the discussion.


Dave Williams, Director of Business Programs for Worldwide Government at Microsoft and is a leader in running hackathons and prompting sessions with public sector organizations, helping them develop and validate Copilot prompts to increase productivity and efficiency.

name: Built in Minutes with GenAI: "My Resume" Website
description:
This workshop showcases how Generative AI and modern developer tools can accelerate personal website creation. Using Cursor (an AI-native IDE) alongside a visual-first hosting platform - developers can scaffold, style, and deploy a responsive resume website in minutes with clean HTML, semantic structure, and production-ready performance.


During this session participants will:


Learn how Gen AI can be used:

  1. layout generation
  2. content structuring
  3. styling suggestions

to build a fully version-controlled, editable, and production ready website!


Presenter Bios


Tejas Ghadge is a senior engineering leader at AWS, managing 100+ engineers across multiple sites, leading development of critical serverless and event-driven services including AWS Lambda Event Source Mappings, AWS Amplify, and AWS EventBridge Pipes. With over a decade of experience architecting large-scale distributed systems processing millions of requests per second, he's a recognized speaker/author on system scalability and high availability. He focuses on building high-performing engineering organizations, fostering innovation, and delivering exceptional customer experiences. He specializes in serverless architectures, event-driven systems, and AI while pushing cloud computing boundaries.


Nithin Vommi is a technology leader with over 8 years of experience at AWS building large scale distributed systems focused on serverless and streaming technologies. He has published technical articles, received patents, and given technical talks at AWS events. Nithin has launched over 12 customer-facing capabilities on serverless platforms that have been featured AWS Re:Invent. His interests include serverless technologies, artificial intelligence, and Internet of things.

name: WorkSource Spring Regional Career Fair
description:
Join us for the Spring Regional Career Fair, taking place on April 29th at Emerald Downs, located at 2300 Ron Crockett Dr, Auburn, WA 98001. This event is the perfect opportunity for you to connect with a wide range of employers from various industries, all eager to find talented individuals like you to join their teams.


By attending the job fair, you will have the chance to:

  1. Network with representatives from leading companies and organizations in the region.
  2. Explore a variety of job openings, from entry-level positions to career-advancing opportunities.
  3. Learn about different industries and discover what roles best suit your skills and interests.


Don't miss out on this valuable chance to take the next step in your career journey. Whether you're looking for your first job, seeking a new opportunity, or wanting to explore different career paths, this job fair has something for everyone.


Plus: free shuttle service between Auburn Station and Emerald Downs!

Auburn Station Bay 6

110 2nd St SW


Register now and mark your calendar for April 29th.

For more information and to secure your spot, please visit our registration page Spring Regional Registration.


We look forward to seeing you there!


Location:

Emerald Downs

2300 Ron Crockett Dr, Auburn, WA 98001

Sponsor: WorkSource

Contact Email: ESDGPWSPierceBusinessServices@esd.wa.gov

Workshop Language Option: English
location: Emerald Downs: 2300 Ron Crockett Dr, Auburn, WA 98001

name: Getting Job Ready with AI
description:
This interactive workshop is designed to help individuals harness the power of Microsoft Copilot (web browser) and M365 Copilot to advance their careers. Participants will learn how to use these AI-driven tools for tasks such as resume building and optimizing, personal branding on LinkedIn, and interview preparation. Through practical exercises and real-world scenarios, attendees will gain valuable skills to enhance their professional profiles and job readiness.


If you'd like to complete some learning before or after the training event, please see Craft effective prompts for Microsoft 365 Copilot.



Note: Please refer to the correct timing for your region, and the session will be recorded.


Recommended for (but not limited to):

Women (all ages and work profiles), returning to work women, and in-house moms residing across ASEAN countries, Australia and New Zealand


Speaker:

Russell Nankervis is a Tech trainer, a Microsoft Certified Professional, a Microsoft Certified Trainer and a valued speaker on Dynamics 365, presenting regularly at D365 User Group and other industry events. Russell is also a Microsoft Gold Partner. With 25-year plus tenure in the IT industry, he has built a reputation for developing strategies, incubating new standards models, and building out development programs.

name: LinkedIn 101: Building a Strong Professional Profile
description:
Facilitator(s)


Aaron Brown and Bharti Gupta, Microsoft Recruiting.


Description


LinkedIn 101: Building a Strong Professional Profile is a practical session designed to help you optimize your LinkedIn presence. Learn key strategies for crafting a compelling profile, showcasing your skills, and expanding your professional network. Whether you're job searching, networking, or building your personal brand, this session will equip you with the tools to stand out on LinkedIn.


Additional Details


This event is part of the AI Workshop Series, which runs every Friday at 1:00pm from April 11th - May 30th (on Zoom) and is brought to you by Microsoft, the Center of Excellence for Information & Computing Technology, Computing For All, and Bellevue College Computer Science.


The AI Workshop Series is open to all community and technical college students across Washington state.

Note: Students must register using their college email address.

name: Future-Proofing Your Career
description:
Join Kai-Lan Chang, Director of Software Engineering at JPMorgan Chase & Co., as he shares tips and tricks for future-proofing your career in the tech industry.


This event is part of our Career Services Workshop Series, designed to provide you with the tools and knowledge needed to excel in your career.

name: Tech@Night: Navigating the Now with SeaCiti
description:
The Tech@Night series brings together tech professionals for an evening of networking, thought-provoking discussions, and community building opportunities. Connect with industry leaders, gain insights into the evolving tech landscape, and engage in meaningful conversations about the future of innovation.


Tech@Night: Navigating the Now will feature a networking mixer and a dynamic panel of Seattle tech professionals discussing career balance, alternative pathways into tech, and how to stay resilient in today’s evolving industry landscape.


Schedule for the Evening

5:30 - 6:20 PM – Networking, refreshments, and Community Pillars showcase.

6:30 - 8:20 PM – Panel discussion featuring industry leaders on career resilience, developing community and leveling up in the tech sector

8:30 - 9:00 PM – Additional networking and refreshments.


Join us for an evening of insightful discussion, authentic community, and fresh perspectives on navigating tech careers in today’s evolving landscape!


Tickets: $20


Light refreshments and two drink tickets included (21+ event, IDs required).


We look forward to seeing you there!


For anyone facing financial limitations, email us at info@seaciti.org for a discounted ticket.
location: Vera Project | 305 Harrison St. Seattle, WA 98109

name: 10 x 10 Jumpstart Your AI Journey
description:
Facilitator(s)


Devindra Chainani is a technology leader with over 30 years of experience in the tech industry, including more than two decades at Microsoft. Currently serving as Director of Technical Program Management for GPU Infrastructure at Oracle, Devindra is deeply engaged in the development of AI infrastructure. He holds multiple patents in the field and is passionate about expanding access to learning through innovation. A master’s candidate at the Harvard Graduate School of Education, Devindra also served on the board of the University of Washington’s Information School and as Co President for the Seattle chapter of Pratham.org which supports innovations in education.


Description


Master the tools that will power the future of work!

This session will give a practical guide to understanding AI tools and how they can be used to advance your education and career. Our speaker will walk you through a 15-day plan to jumpstart your AI readiness.


Additional Details


This event is part of the AI Workshop Series, which runs every Friday at 1:00pm from April 11th - May 30th (on Zoom) and is brought to you by Microsoft, the Center of Excellence for Information & Computing Technology, Computing For All, and Bellevue College Computer Science.


The AI Workshop Series is open to all community and technical college students across Washington state.

Note: Students must register using their college email address.

name: Seattle Tech Career Fair: Exclusive Tech Hiring Event
description:
Tech Career Fair focuses on helping companies achieve their diversity and inclusivity initiative with more diverse non-traditional candidates


We will be hosting a Tech Career Fair with our hiring partners from fast growing startups and Fortune 500 companies in technology. There will be a focus on helping companies achieve their diversity and inclusivity initiative with more diverse candidates to their talent pool. Available roles that our hiring companies are looking to fill are of the following:


  1. Software Engineering
  2. Product Management
  3. Data Scientist
  4. AI/Machine Learning Engineer
  5. Data Analyst
  6. UI/UX Design
  7. Marketing
  8. Sales


To make sure your profile is visible and accessible to hiring partners and companies in their system please make sure you complete your profile and upload your resume to the platform.


Steps:

  1. Complete the Sign-up form here or the link below and upload your resume to the system to get your QR code/candidate profile link: https://careerscrossroad.com/signup?code=SeattleTCF
  2. Improve your profile while you are waiting for your profile to be approved* Completed and approved profiles will then be visible to different companies for each career fair where companies will then select who to send the invitation to attend their career fair with the next steps.
  3. Follow the instructions in your email invitation from the employers and attend the career fair with your QR code/digital profile link with the hiring companies that you have been assigned to.



*Note: This is an "invite-only" event due to limited space and high demand. Signing up on Eventbrite alone does not give you access to the event. You would still need to apply with the steps above for a potential invitation. Only shortlisted candidates will then be notified for the next steps on how to attend. Please make sure you complete your profile as completely as you can and upload your resume to the platform for approval(If the reviewer feels your profile is incomplete, they might not approve it in order to maintain the quality of candidates being presented to the companies). Approval process timeline varies depending on the backlog. Once approved, your profile will be visible and accessible to hiring partners and companies on the platform. To increase your chances to be selected by one of our hiring companies and partners to one of the hiring events, it is recommended that you constantly refine and improve your profile.

name: Beyond the Resume: How to Create Your Action Plan for Success
description:
Facilitator(s)


Tina-Marie Gulley, CEO of Ada Developers Academy


Description


In today’s job market, a resume alone won’t cut it. This workshop dives into the real skills and strategies you need to succeed—building a professional network, gaining hands-on experience, and upskilling continuously. Whether you’re a student, career-changer, or re-entering the workforce, ADA Developers Academy will show you how to take charge of your career journey, embrace lifelong learning, and confidently navigate the tech industry.


Additional Details


This event is part of the AI Workshop Series, which runs every Friday at 1:00pm from April 11th - May 30th (on Zoom) and is brought to you by Microsoft, the Center of Excellence for Information & Computing Technology, Computing For All, and Bellevue College Computer Science.


The AI Workshop Series is open to all community and technical college students across Washington state.

Note: Students must register using their college email address.
